headline: Gen Z credit scores are suffering as they open more credit cards
summary: Gen Z consumers — and spenders — are opening more credit card accounts at higher rates than any other generation. The one big drawback is that their credit scores are paying for it. Yahoo Finance Senior Columnist Kerry Hannon explains how Gen Z credit scores are suffering, according to a new FICO report, as they utilize credit cards as a sort of financial cushion for paying bills.

headline: Gen Z is opening more credit cards and seeing their credit scores drop
summary: More than 25% of Gen Z adults between 18 and 29 with a FICO score opened at least one credit card in the past year — the highest rate of any age group,
